response.justification: Candidate is an experienced full-stack engineer with deep TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang background, but the role demands a hands-on C++ specialist with industrial automation context. His only direct C/C++ experience is a ~6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago, with no demonstrated work in IEC 62443, MQTT/Modbus/OPC UA, message brokers, or industrial cybersecurity. Strong general engineering skill is present, but the core technical fit for this specific C++/industrial role is weak.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded
Solid track record with testing discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentoring/teaching
Some low-level systems exposure via DLang microservices, async I/O, and prior C/C++ work at National Instruments
concerns: No recent, substantive C++/STL production experience — last C++ role was ~10 years ago and brief
No evidence of industrial automation, IEC 62443/SDL, MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, or message-broker architectures
Career trajectory has moved toward web/cloud/AI tooling, not the on-prem industrial C++ platform space

key_factors.factor: Missing industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443) and protocol expertise
Strong general engineering and systems thinking
